query: Ciena job posting (artificial intelligence OR machine learning OR generative AI OR LLM OR data scientist)

results.title: Ciena AI Software Engineer Jobs 2026 Freshers
highlights:
# Ciena Hiring AI Software Engineer 2026 | Freshers Apply Now for Gurugram
[...]
Ciena, a global leader in high-speed connectivity and intelligent networking solutions, is hiring freshers for the role of AI Software Engineer at its Gurugram office. This entry-level position is part of Ciena’s 2026 recruitment drive and offers a unique opportunity to work on production-grade AI systems that power next-generation network infrastructure. As the telecom industry increasingly adopts AI for automation and optimization, Ciena is investing heavily in AI-driven software engineering, making this role a strong entry point for engineers looking to build a career at the intersection of AI and networking. The role emphasizes hands-on work with Python, RESTful APIs, machine learning pipelines, and large language models (LLMs), providing freshers with exposure to modern AI stacks and microservice architectures.
[...]
| Company | Ciena |
| --- | --- |
| Role | AI Software Engineer |
| Location | Gurugram, India |
| Experience | 0–1 Years |
| Education | Bachelor's or Master's in CS, AI, ML or related field |
| Salary | As per Company Standards |
[...]
Candidates must hold a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, or a related quantitative field. While the job is open to freshers (0–1 years of experience), the company expects a solid foundation in Python with object-oriented programming, experience building RESTful APIs (FastAPI preferred), and familiarity with data processing tools like Pandas or SQL. A background in machine learning techniques and exposure to LLMs or orchestration frameworks through projects or internships is highly valued. Although the original job snippet mentions ‘Bachelor’s degree in any engineering discipline from Tier I (IIT/NIT) schools’, the detailed description clarifies that the role is specifically for CS/AI/ML graduates. Candidates from Tier-1 institutes will have an edge, but strong project work can compensate for non-Tier-1 backgrounds.
[...]
- Design and develop modular Python services for AI platforms.
- Build and extend RESTful APIs using FastAPI in microservice architectures.
- Implement AI workflows using LangChain and LangGraph.
- Apply ML techniques across data prep, model training, and validation.
- Experiment with prompt engineering to improve model performance.
- Debug and troubleshoot AI/ML systems for production reliability.
- Collaborate with engineering and data teams to deliver scalable AI features.
[...]
- Python with OOP
- RESTful APIs (FastAPI)
- Data Processing (Pandas, SQL)
- Machine Learning Techniques
- LLMs and Orchestration (LangChain, LangGraph)
- Cloud Platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP)
- Docker, Kubernetes
- Networking Concepts (TCP/IP, SDN, NFV)
- Vector Databases
[...]
The selection process typically begins with application screening, followed by a technical assessment that may include coding challenges on Python, API development, and basic ML concepts. Shortlisted candidates then face a technical interview round focusing on problem-solving, system design for microservices, and hands-on knowledge of AI frameworks. A final HR round assesses cultural fit, communication skills, and alignment with Ciena’s people-first values. The process can take 2–4 weeks. Given the niche AI requirements, candidates with strong GitHub profiles or internship projects demonstrating LLM integration will have a significant advantage.
[...]
Freshers (0–1 years experience) with a Bachelor’s or Master’s in CS, AI, ML, or related fields. Strong Python, ML, and API skills are required. Tier-1 institute graduates may have an advantage, but strong projects can compensate.
[...]
Click the ‘Apply Link’ below to visit Ciena’s official career portal. Ensure your resume highlights Python, ML, and any AI project work. Apply before the link expires, as applications are reviewed on a rolling basis.

results.title: Full Stack Developer | Hirevector
highlights:
Full Stack Developer | Hirevector

Hirevector.← All Jobs

# Full Stack Developer

Posted May 30, 2026

As the global leader in high-speed connectivity, Ciena is committed to a people-first approach. Our teams enjoy a culture focused on prioritizing a flexible work environment that empowers individual growth, well-being, and belonging. We’re a technology company that leads with our humanity—driving our business priorities alongside meaningful social, community, and societal impact. As a Full Stack Developer, this role creates meaningful impact by transforming complex data workflows into intuitive, scalable, and secure web applications that support Ciena’s mission of building the world’s most adaptive networks. The position contributes directly to modernizing legacy processes, enhancing user experience, and enabling data‑driven decision‑making across the organization. How you will make an impact: • Design and develop responsive front‑end applications using ReactJS or AngularJS, transforming complex data flows into intuitive user experiences. • Build and maintain robust backend services with Python frameworks such as Django or Flask. • Convert legacy spreadsheet‑based workflows into scalable, interactive web applications. • Integrate RESTful APIs and manage data interactions with platforms including Snowflake and PostgreSQL. • Partner with UX/UI designers, product managers, and cross‑functional engineering teams to deliver high‑quality solutions. • Apply secure coding practices, performance optimization techniques, and CI/CD methodologies. • Participate in code reviews and contribute to continuous improvement initiatives. The must haves: • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, MIS, Business, IT, or a related field, or a minimum of 5 years of relevant technical experience. • Proven experience in full‑stack application development and solution design. • Proficiency with ReactJS, AngularJS, Python, and responsive UI development. • Experience working with RESTful APIs, MySQL, PostgreSQL, Snowflake, and Git. • Strong understanding of Agile/Scrum methodologies. • Technical expertise across HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, and Python frameworks such as Django or Flask. • Experience working with AWS cloud architecture and DevOps practices. Nice to haves: • Experience modernizing Excel‑based processes into web applications. • Strong debugging and problem‑solving skills across the full application stack. • Prior work on application performance tuning and secure coding practices. • Knowledge of data security concepts. • Familiarity with emerging technologies and a passion for continuous learning. • Strong communication, collaboration, and stakeholder management capabilities. • Experience with CI/CD pipeline optimization. At Ciena, we are committed to building and fostering an environment in which our employees feel respected, valued, and heard. Ciena values the diversity of its workforce and respects its employees as individuals. We do not tolerate any form of discrimination. Ciena is an Equal Opportunity Employer, including disability and protected veteran status. If contacted in relation to a job opportunity, please advise Ciena of any accommodation measures you may require.

Apply for This Job

results.title: Business and Data Analyst Intern (Summer 2026) | Hirevector
highlights:
Business and Data Analyst Intern (Summer 2026) | Hirevector

Hirevector.← All Jobs

# Business and Data Analyst Intern (Summer 2026)

Posted May 30, 2026

As the global leader in high-speed connectivity, Ciena is committed to a people-first approach. Our teams enjoy a culture focused on prioritizing a flexible work environment that empowers individual growth, well-being, and belonging. We’re a technology company that leads with our humanity—driving our business priorities alongside meaningful social, community, and societal impact. Job Title: Business & Data Analyst Intern Job Overview: Ciena Corporation is seeking an inquisitive, detail-oriented intern who is excited about working at the intersection of business strategy and data-driven insights. This role will involve not only analyzing data but also understanding how data can inform and drive business decisions. You will gain valuable experience on both the business side – learning key metrics, objectives, & strategic priorities – and the data side, where you will develop the skills to gather, interpret, analyze, manipulate, and model data to support business goals. Key Responsibilities: Assist in collecting, manipulating, modeling, analyzing, and visualizing data to support business decisions, ensuring alignment between business needs and data-driven insights. Help develop reports and dashboards to monitor key business metrics, translating data findings into meaningful business outcomes. Collaborate with both business and data teams to identify trends, opportunities, and insights that can inform strategic decision-making and drive process improvement. Support the creation of ad-hoc reports and presentations for leadership, helping bridge the gap between technical data and business strategy. Learn and utilize new data tools and applications, while also gaining a strong understanding of how these tools support business objectives. Qualifications: Currently pursuing a degree in Business, Data Analytics, or a related field. Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel (formulas, pivots, charts) & a knack for data analysis. Excellent analytical skills, attention to detail, and ability to see the bigger business picture. Eagerness to learn how to balance and sync both the business and data perspectives to drive decision-making. Effective communicator with the ability to present complex data and information clearly to non-technical business stakeholders. Excitement to learn and apply new project management tools and methods. Ideal Candidate: The ideal candidate is curious about both business and data, eager to develop a hybrid skill set, and passionate about using data to drive business strategy. Pay Range: The hourly pay range for this position is $21.50 - $29.00 Pay ranges at Ciena are designed to accommodate variations in knowledge, skills, experience, market conditions, and locations, reflecting our diverse products, industries, and lines of business. Please note that the pay range information provided in this posting pertains specifically to the primary location, which is the top location listed in case multiple locations are available. In addition to competitive compensation, Ciena offers students access to the Employee Assistance Program (EAP), company-paid holidays, paid sick leave, and vacation pay as required by applicable laws. At Ciena, we are committed to building and fostering an environment in which our employees feel respected, valued, and heard. Ciena values the diversity of its workforce and respects its employees as individuals. We do not tolerate any form of discrimination. Ciena is an Equal Opportunity Employer, including disability and protected veteran status. If contacted in relation to a job opportunity, please advise Ciena of any accommodation measures you may require.

Apply for This Job

highlights:
Business Analyst – RMA Spend Cube | Hirevector
[...]
# Business Analyst – RMA Spend Cube
[...]
Job Overview: Ciena Corporation is seeking an experienced Business Analyst to join our Service Operations organization. This individual will serve as the lead developer and operator of the RMA Spend Cube, a single source of truth analytics platform enabling multi-dimensional spend visibility and cost reduction insights across suppliers, categories, and regions.
[...]
This role is responsible for designing, building, automating, and refining complex data pipelines and business logic that transform raw data into scalable, trusted insights. It requires someone who can independently architect and maintain data-driven solutions while deeply understanding the business context needed to support operational decision-making and enterprise-level analytics.
[...]
- Lead development and ongoing operation of the RMA Spend Cube, ensuring it serves as a reliable, scalable, single source of truth for spend and analytics.
[...]
- Build, automate, and optimize data pipelines that integrate and transform data from multiple sources into clean, structured datasets for analysis.
[...]
- Design and implement complex business logic and transformation layers (e.g., multi-layer rules, calculations, and reconciliations) that enable automated reporting and insights.
[...]
- Continuously enhance automation and tooling to improve efficiency, reduce manual effort, and scale analytics capabilities across the organization.
[...]
Required Skills & Experience:
[...]
- Experience with ETL, data integration, and data transformation processes, including familiarity with tools like Pentaho Data Integration (a plus).
- Ability to analyze and interpret complex datasets, automate data processes, and assess business relevance.
[...]
- Proficiency in scripting and programming for data analysis and logic implementation (e.g., SQL); advanced Excel capabilities.
[...]
- Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to think critically about data connections, structure, and business context.
[...]
- Effective communication skills
[...]
Preferred Qualifications:
[...]
- Experience with Pentaho Data Integration, Snowflake, Oracle, MySQL, VBA, and other scripting languages.
- Experience building or maintaining centralized data products, analytics platforms, or “single source of truth” systems.
- Exposure to automation of business processes through data-driven logic and workflows.
- Familiarity with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, ThoughtSpot).

results.title: Chief Information Officer | Hireza
highlights: . Position Overview Reporting directly to the Chief Financial Officer and serving as a key member of Ciena’s extended leadership team, the Chief Information Officer (CIO) is the executive accountable for the vision, strategy, and execution of all enterprise technology and digital capabilities that power the company’s growth, agility, and competitive advantage. The CIO leads a global IT organization responsible for internal applications, infrastructure, architecture, data, analytics, collaboration platforms, and AI-enabled productivity tools. This is a highly strategic and business-oriented role that demands a proven technology leader who combines deep technical credibility with exceptional business acumen, influencing skills, and the ability to operate as a true partner to the many various stakeholders including R&D, Supply Chain, Finance, and Sales and Marketing. Key Responsibilities Enterprise IT Strategy & Business Partnership • Develop and execute a forward-looking IT roadmap that directly accelerates revenue growth, margin expansion, product development velocity, and operational excellence. • Serve as the primary technology advisor to the Executive Leadership Team and Board of Directors, translating complex technical concepts into clear business impact and ROI. • Collaborating with our Enterprise Transformation Office, to support transformation initiatives that leverage AI, generative AI, automation, and advanced analytics to re-imagine R&D workflows, supply-chain resilience, sales effectiveness, and customer support. Global Infrastructure & Operations (Compute, Storage, Network) • Own the strategy, architecture, and 24×7 operations of all on-premises and cloud-based infrastructure supporting R&D labs (high-performance compute clusters, optical/electrical test beds, simulation environments), corporate functions, and remote employees. • Ensure ultra-reliable, low-latency global networking that supports 40% fully remote workforce and geographically dispersed engineering teams. • Partnering with the CISO, Lead the evolution toward hybrid/multi-cloud environments, software-defined data centers, and zero-trust security architectures. Applications, Collaboration & AI Ecosystem • Oversee the full application portfolio (PLM, ERP, CRM, HCM, CPQ, custom engineering tools) with a relentless focus on simplification, consolidation, and user experience. • Champion an industry-leading collaboration and productivity platform strategy (ie, Microsoft 365 Copilot, Miro, AI-assisted code review, virtual lab access, etc.) tailored to distributed R&D and remote-first culture. • Partner to build and govern a next gen enterprise AI approach that enables responsible use of generative AI across code generation, documentation, test-case creation, failure analysis, and customer-facing knowledge systems. Enterprise Architecture, Data Strategy & Analytics • Define and enforce a modern, modular enterprise architecture that supports rapid integration of acquired technologies and scalability to 10x growth. • Lead the company’s data strategy: master data management, data mesh principles, real-time analytics, and predictive insights for R&D yield, supply-chain risk, and market intelligence. • Deliver executive dashboards and AI-driven decision support tools that provide a single source of truth for the leadership team. Cybersecurity, Risk & Compliance • Ensure world-class cybersecurity posture (zero trust, AI-powered threat detection, secure-by-design lab environments) commensurate with serving the world’s largest cloud and telecom operators. • Maintain compliance with SOX, ISO 27001, GDPR, and emerging data-sovereignty regulations across all operating geographies. Team Leadership & Talent Development • Build and lead a high-performing, globally distributed IT organization that attracts top engineering and architecture talent. • Foster a culture of innovation, ownership, and continuous improvement while maintaining

highlights:
Director, Strategic Transactions at Ciena - United States Remote
[...]
# Director, Strategic Transactions
[...]
🇺🇸 United States – Remote
[...]
Ciena is a global leader in optical and routing systems, services, and automation software. We build the world’s most adaptive networks to address ever-increasing digital demands for richer, more connected experiences for all users. For three-plus decades, we’ve brought our innate sense of humanity to our relentless pursuit of innovation.
[...]
### 📋 Description
[...]
• Partner closely with Chief Strategy Office and Legal leadership across all stages of acquisition transactions, including non-disclosure agreements, term sheets and letters of intent, deal structuring, running deal processes, drafting and negotiating definitive documents, closing deals and supporting integration and post-closing matters • Lead legal due diligence processes and coordinate with other functions to communicate findings, risks and recommended solutions to stakeholders • Play a key communication and coordination role across diverse functions and levels to complete transactions • Develop strategies and tools to drive continuous improvements in deal processes and execution, including by utilizing AI • Support corporate venture capital program and investments, including management of related outside counsel relationships and engagements • Develop legal strategies and efficient processes to coordinate and oversee such investments and ongoing portfolio management • Play a leading role in negotiating and drafting of strategic commercial and technology transactions and partnerships • Assist the broader Legal Department and Chief Strategy Officer on other high priority tasks and other corporate or transactional matters
[...]
### 🎯 Requirements
[...]
• 5+ years of legal experience in M&A, with a preference for strategic acquisition transactions and some in-house experience or outside counsel work that includes a recurring buy-side relationship with an acquisitive client • Experience independently negotiating and running complex strategic transactions • Ability to think creatively, function independently and work proactively in a fast-paced environment • Demonstrated growth mindset and strong initiative—ambitious, adaptable, and willing to take ownership and tackle unfamiliar challenges with confidence • Excellent legal and business judgment, attention to detail and proven capacity to tackle complicated problems • Strong process orientation with a passion for continuous improvement and strong comfort with the use of AI and other technologies to support transactions • Team player with willingness to adapt and support the diverse needs of a dynamic legal department • Effective communicator with stakeholders and executives • Active bar membership (in good standing) in your state of residency, with eligibility to practice as in-house counsel.

results.title: Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
highlights:
Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
[...]
# Data Scientist
[...]
Optum is seeking a Data Science professional who is passionate about applying machine learning, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex healthcare problems on a scale. Imagine working with massive volumes of healthcare data spanning clinical, claims, behavioral, and digital interactions – powering intelligent personalization, discovery, and decision support for millions of members, providers, and care teams.
[...]
At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.
[...]
Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. By joining Optum’s data science team, you will be part of an exciting journey to build advanced ML and GenAI driven products, grounded in responsible AI principles, that operate at enterprise scale and create meaningful impact across the healthcare ecosystem.
[...]
- Design, develop, and deploy advanced analytics, machine learning, and Generative AI solutions to address complex healthcare and business problems using largescale structured and unstructured data
[...]
- Applies advanced techniques including statistics, machine learning, simulation, and mathematical modelling
[...]
- Build and optimize LLM based applications, including Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) frameworks, to enable accurate, context aware, and explainable insights from enterprise healthcare data sources such as clinical notes, claims, provider data, and policy documents
[...]
- Develop, evaluate, and finetune machine learning and GenAI models, including prompt engineering, retrieval strategies, and model evaluation techniques to improve accuracy, relevance, and performance
[...]
- Support prototyping, validation, and deployment of GenAI and ML solutions, working with engineering teams to ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable. Continuously stay current with advancements in LLMs, GenAI frameworks, and healthcare AI trends, contributing innovative ideas and improvements to Optum’s capabilities
[...]
### Required Qualifications:
[...]
- Bachelor’s Degree (preferably in information technology, engineering, math, computer science, analytics, engineering or other related field)
- 4+ years of experience in handling/active development in data science projects
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in Microsoft Azure Cloud, Databricks, Mlflow, Spark SQL & Python
[...]
- Experience using Machine Learning Algorithms: Linear & Logistic Regression, Time Series analysis, Support Vector Machine, K-Means clustering, K-Nearest Neighbors Decision Trees, Random Forest, Naive Bayes, PCA, SVD, Artificial Neural Networks, Association Rules, Genetic Algorithm, Optimization, Bagging and Boosting algorithms etc.

results.title: This Is My Best Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock to Buy in June (Hint: It's Not Micron Technology) | The Motley Fool
highlights:
So, don't be surprised to see Micron stock getting a nice shot in the arm following its upcoming report in June, especially considering that it trades at a really attractive valuation. However, there's another high-flying company that's set to release its quarterly report on June 4 -- Ciena (CIEN 8.59%). Its shares have jumped by 136% in 2026, and the incredible growth that it has been delivering could give it a nice boost next month.
[...]
Let's see why Ciena is my top AI stock to buy in June, even after the stunning gains it has delivered so far this year.
[...]
## Like Micron, Ciena is an important player in the AI infrastructure ecosystem
[...]
Micron's high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips help AI data centers overcome a key bottleneck. These specialized memory chips continuously feed large datasets to AI processors quickly and power-efficiently, thereby reducing latency and enabling AI model training and inference applications to run seamlessly.
[...]
The faster data access enabled by HBM means AI processors in data centers won't need to sit idle and waste resources. Ciena's optical networking components also help address this bottleneck by enabling high-speed data transfer in AI data center clusters. The massive size of AI models means that multiple AI data centers and servers need to be linked together through high-speed networks.
[...]
Ciena's optical components power these data center interconnects (DCI). Precedence Research estimates that the global data center interconnect market could grow from just under $19 billion this year to almost $65 billion in 2035, at an annual rate of almost 15%. Meanwhile, the demand for optical networking components could increase at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 21% over the next five years to support fast connections in AI data centers, according to market research provider Cignal AI.
[...]
This explains why Ciena has been growing at an incredible pace. The company's revenue increased by 33% year over year in the first quarter of fiscal 2026 (which ended on Jan. 31, 2026) to $1.43 billion. The company anticipates full-year revenue to increase by 28% to $6.1 billion at the midpoint of its guidance range, an improvement over the 19% growth it clocked in the previous fiscal year.
[...]
What's more, the AI data center-fueled demand is leading to higher profitability for Ciena. Its adjusted earnings increased by 111% year over year in fiscal Q1. The good part is that the strong pricing power it enjoys in the optical transport industry is expected to drive further margin gains in the second half of the fiscal year. Ciena notes that demand for its products is outpacing supply, leading to a jump in prices.
[...]
In fact, the company expects demand to be higher than supply "at least
[...]
could be in the
[...]
. Another important point is that Ciena received $2 billion in orders in
[...]
Ciena is trading at a whopping 372 times trailing earnings. However, its forward earnings multiple of 128 points toward a big jump in its earnings. Of course, Ciena's forward earnings multiple is also on the expensive side. However, its premium can be justified by a potential 136% increase in its earnings this fiscal year, according to Yahoo! Finance.
[...]
Don't be surprised to see Ciena sustaining its healthy earnings growth rate beyond the next three fiscal years. That's because massive investments in AI data centers are poised to continue through the end of the decade, with McKinsey expecting a whopping $5.2 trillion in annual capital spending in 2030. As Ciena provides key components that help AI data centers operate smoothly, it is likely to continue to witness robust top- and bottom-line growth over the long run.
[...]
Just as Micron's memory chips play an essential role in AI data centers, Ciena's networking components do the same.
[...]
Ciena's revenue and earnings growth have been quite strong in recent quarters, and there is ample evidence that it will continue to grow.

results.title: Ciena's $50 Billion AI Network Target Puts Optical Capacity on the Hyperscaler Clock | SendTech Times
highlights:
# Ciena's $50 Billion AI Network Target Puts Optical Capacity on the Hyperscaler Clock
[...]
Ciena says AI demand could roughly double its addressable market to about $50 billion by 2029 as hyperscalers and service providers invest in optical networking. It cited RLS Hyper Rail, DCOM, coherent modules and 400G/800G pluggable optics as demand areas while planning $250 million to $275 million in capex this year. The practical test is whether AI compute buildouts convert into durable network orders.
[...]
Ciena's latest earnings commentary turns the AI infrastructure boom into a test for optical networking, not only compute capacity.
[...]
According to RCR Wireless News, the company said AI-driven demand could approximately double its addressable market to roughly $50 billion by 2029, with spending spreading across wide-area networks and data center networks.
[...]
Ciena president and chief executive Gary Smith told investors that the largest hyperscalers had increased 2026 capital expenditure plans, with indications of expansion into 2027 and beyond.
[...]
His argument is that companies spending heavily on AI compute will need more network capacity to move data for training, ingestion and inference.
[...]
Ciena is framing the next bottleneck as high-capacity, low-latency connectivity across long-haul routes, metro networks and data center interconnects.
[...]
If that view holds, optical systems could capture a larger share of the AI budget as customers try to monetize constrained compute investments.
[...]
RLS Hyper Rail, Ciena's next-generation intelligent line system, was developed with multiple hyperscalers and is aimed at capacity and efficiency requirements around data centers.
[...]
Smith said Ciena had received what he described as the industry's first multirail order from a leading hyperscaler for the platform.
[...]
RLS Hyper
[...]
long routes measured
[...]
to extend capacity.
[...]
The signal is narrow but important: Ciena has an early hyperscaler order, while additional hyperscalers, neocloud providers and service providers are still in evaluation discussions.
[...]
Ciena also cited momentum in Data Center Out-of-Band Management, or DCOM, which combines routing and switching products with passive optical network technology.
[...]
Smith said DCOM contributed to 88% year-on-year revenue growth, and that Ciena had initial orders from a second hyperscaler customer in addition to Meta, with a third hyperscaler moving through lab qualification.
[...]
Ciena also sees carriers returning to network spending after a restrained investment period, with openings in long-haul, metro and managed optical fiber networks.
[...]
That makes the forecast relevant to carriers trying to support AI traffic without turning every investment cycle into a full data center buildout.
[...]
The company also reported a new win with a major hyperscaler for high-performance coherent modules.
[...]
Those modules are expected to be deployed across metro and long-haul data center interconnect networks that support both WAN and data center applications.
[...]
Management also expects 400G and 800G pluggable optics to remain a growth line, with pluggable revenue projected to exceed twice the 2025 level.
[...]
A $50 billion addressable-market target does not by itself prove that AI networking demand will spread evenly across vendors or customers.
[...]
The evidence to watch is whether Ciena's early hyperscaler wins expand into repeat deployments, whether service-provider reinvestment continues, and whether DCOM's growth can hold after the first wave of hyperscaler adoption.
[...]
Ciena is preparing for that demand with spending of $250 million to $275 million in capital expenditures this year, including manufacturing and supply-chain capacity.
[...]
The key question is whether optical networking becomes a sustained part of AI infrastructure budgets, or whether the current demand is concentrated in a small number of aggressive hyperscale builds.

results.title: Engr III Spec-AI Science, Chennai, India | Verizon Careers
highlights:
Engr III Spec-AI Science, Chennai, India | Verizon Careers

Data Intelligence

# Engr III Spec-AI Science

- Chennai, India
- Hyderabad, India

Job ID: r-1095372

Apply prior to the end date: June 30, 2026

### When you join Verizon

You want more out of a career. A place to share your ideas freely — even if they’re daring or different. Where the true you can learn, grow, and thrive. At Verizon, we power and empower how people live, work and play by connecting them to what brings them joy. We do what we love — driving innovation, creativity, and impact in the world. Our V Team is a community of people who anticipate, lead, and believe that listening is where learning begins. In crisis and in celebration, we come together — lifting our communities and building trust in how we show up, everywhere & always. Want in? Join the #VTeamLife.

What you'll be doing…

As an Engineer III Specialist in AI Science, you will be a key hands-on contributor responsible for the implementation, testing, and maintenance of AI/ML solutions that power the autonomous network operations platform. This role supports the core strategy of transitioning from reactive, queue-based work to continuous, 24/7 autonomous network coverage. You will be responsible for developing and deploying production-grade AI driven Agents that operate within the five layers of autonomous agents, focusing on data processing, feature engineering, and implementation of algorithms that detect issues, aggregate impact, and generate evidence-based insights.

Develop and implement components of AI/ML solutions using Python and CI/CD practices.

Contribute to building scalable and efficient AI Agents for large-scale data processing.

Implement and analyze the performance of established algorithms, including those from deep learning and generative AI.

Write complex queries using SQL/BigQuery to extract data and build features necessary to train models and derive business insights.

Assist in designing and implementing AI/ML pipelines for model training and deployment.

Collaborate effectively with team members to deliver high-quality, impactful autonomous solutions.

What we're looking for…

You'll need to have:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, or a related field or four or more year of work experience.

Three or more yeas of relevant work experience.

Two or more years of experience developing and implementing AI/ML solutions.

Strong programming skills in Python and Spark, with experience in CI/CD practices.

Experience with public cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and distributed computing.

Understanding of AI/ML algorithms, including deep learning and generative AI techniques.

Experience working with GCP, BigQuery/SQL, Python, and Spark.

Even better if you have one or more of the following:

Experience in building scoring models and anomaly detection solutions.

Experience with developing and deploying machine learning models to production.

Familiarity with the telecommunications industry data and network domain.

If Verizon and this role sound like a fit for you, we encourage you to apply even if you don’t meet every “even better” qualification listed above.

### Where you’ll be working

In this hybrid role, you'll have a defined work location that includes work from home and assigned office days set by your manager.

### Scheduled Weekly Hours

40

### Equal Employment Opportunity

Verizon is an equal opportunity employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to race, gender, disability or any other legally protected characteristics.

results.title: Sr Engr Cslt-Data Science, Madhapur, India | Verizon Careers
highlights:
Sr Engr Cslt-Data Science, Madhapur, India | Verizon Careers
[...]
# Sr Engr Cslt-Data Science
[...]
- Hyderabad, India
- Bangalore, India
- Chennai, India
[...]
As a Consultant (Analytics) you will be a pivotal player in modernizing telecom financial operations. Bridging the gap between implementation and architectural strategy, you will design, develop, and deploy advanced AI/ML, Generative AI, and Agentic AI solutions tailored for the finance domain. Your work will transition complex financial workflows—such as Forecasting as a Service, Explainability framework, Funnel Forecasting, Customer Profitability etc., and also performing analyses that impact Bad Debt, Cash flow, Credit Score cut models, etc., among others—into automated, intelligent, and proactive systems.
[...]
You will own the end-to-end lifecycle of critical AI models while translating complex technical results into compelling business narratives for finance leadership.
[...]
Design and deploy core AI/ML and Generative AI solutions to optimize financial operations.
[...]
Build and orchestrate Agentic AI workflows to automate multi-step financial analysis and decision-making processes.
[...]
Utilize foundational models and AI assistants (e.g., Claude, GitHub Copilot) to rapidly prototype, iterate, and deliver agile AI solutions to meet dynamic business needs.
[...]
Engineer robust data pipelines and write complex SQL/BigQuery queries to extract and transform large-scale financial datasets using GCP and Python.
[...]
Act as a data storyteller—synthesizing complex AI/ML model outputs into clear, actionable, and prescriptive insights for non-technical finance stakeholders.
[...]
Implement MLOps best practices to ensure continuous integration, deployment, and monitoring of financial AI models in production environments.
[...]
Driving data-derived insights across the business domain by developing advanced statistical models, machine learning algorithms and computational algorithms based on business initiatives.
[...]
Your primary focus will be transforming traditional financial operations into a proactive, AI-driven organization. You are a hands-on builder who thrives at the intersection of core ML, cutting-edge GenAI, and business strategy. You have a knack for rapid prototyping and possess the communication skills required to explain the "why" and "how" of AI to business leaders.
[...]
You'll need to have:
[...]
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field or four or more years of work experience.
[...]
Three or more years of experience practicing data science, AI engineering, or machine learning, with a strong focus on core AI/ML, GenAI, and Agentic frameworks.
[...]
Experience with all phases of end-to-end Analytics project, such as (but not limited to): Ingestion, Munging, Model Building, Validation, Operationalization, Monitoring
[...]
Strong programming proficiency in Python and advanced SQL.
[...]
Proven experience working within cloud platforms (Teradata/ GCP) and using tools like BigQuery for large-scale data processing.
[...]
Demonstrated experience in rapid prototyping and building solutions leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs) and AI tools (e.g., Claude, Copilot).
[...]
Exceptional data storytelling and communication skills, with a proven ability to translate complex technical concepts into confident financial business decisions.
[...]
Advanced degree (Master’s) in Computer Science, Statistics, Data Science, or a related field.
[...]
Hands-on familiarity with specific Agentic AI and orchestration frameworks (e.g., LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex).
[...]
Experience in designing and deploying end-to-end MLOps and LLMOps pipelines.
[...]
A strong understanding of financial forecasting models, anomaly detection algorithms, and time-series analysis.

results.title: Remote AI/ML Engineering at Cint - Jobicy
highlights:
Remote AI/ML Engineering at Cint - Jobicy
[...]
Cint is hiring an AI/ML Engineer to build and maintain production ML systems for its global research technology marketplace. The role involves developing services like feasibility prediction and dynamic pricing, owning end-to-end cloud deployments, and collaborating with product and data science teams. The position offers high autonomy, AI-native tooling, and a modern tech stack including Python, AWS, and K8s. Based in Prague with remote options, the role requires strong Python skills, ML knowledge, and experience with LLMs.
[...]
AI Insight The role requires expertise in both traditional ML and LLMs, production system ownership, and collaboration across teams, making it challenging but
[...]
entry-level
[...]
Python Machine Learning AWS LLMs SQL Databricks Kubernetes FastAPI CI/CD Data Science
[...]
I am excited to apply for the AI/ML Engineer role at Cint. With strong proficiency in Python and hands-on experience building production ML systems on AWS, I am well-prepared to contribute to your marketplace's feasibility prediction and dynamic pricing models. My background includes fine-tuning LLMs and deploying scalable services using Docker and Kubernetes, aligning with your modern tech stack.
[...]
I thrive in high-autonomy environments and enjoy owning services end-to-end, from PoC to cloud deployment. I am also experienced in collaborating with data scientists and product managers to translate business questions into impactful ML solutions.
[...]
I am particularly drawn to Cint's AI-native engineering culture and the opportunity to work with Claude Code to accelerate development. I look forward to bringing my analytical skills and passion for machine learning to your team.
[...]
real-time
[...]
for a recommendation
[...]
We’re hiring an AI/ML Engineer for our AI/ML team in Prague to work on the production ML and data science systems that power Cint’s marketplace. These are data products that our research customers depend on every day, including:
[...]
Build and maintain production ML systems: Contribute to existing ML-based services, from training pipelines to production performance optimizations.
[...]
Own services end-to-end: Design, build and maintain production services – from a PoC all the way to a functional cloud deployment.
[...]
Partner with product and data science: Work with PMs, data scientists, and other engineers to translate research and business questions into shippable ML.
[...]
Understand the platform: To facilitate the above, you’ll work in Databricks, AWS and other tooling to be able to analyze, understand and present the data, metrics and insights needed to help make the best decisions for the platform.
[...]
Leverage AI tooling: Use Claude Code and agentic programming tools to accelerate the boilerplate so you can focus on the modelling decisions that actually matter.
[...]
Excellent programming skills and proficiency in Python. Knowledge of Java is a plus.
[...]
Knowledge of traditional machine learning tools and techniques (e. g. support vector machines, gradient boosting)
[...]
An understanding of LLMs and their architecture, ideally with experience in fine-tuning, e.g. LoRA, distillation is a plus
[...]
Proficient in SQL, knowledge of Databricks is a plus
[...]
Familiarity with cloud technologies, e.g. AWS
[...]
Proven experience as an AI/ML Engineer
[...]
Prague-First, Europe-Friendly: Our preferred base is Prague, alongside our existing AI/ML team. Remote work from Spain or the UK is also possible, as these are the markets where we have entities.
[...]
AI-Native Engineering: We’re rolling out Claude Code and modern agentic tooling across engineering. You’ll use it daily, not as a novelty, but as a force multiplier for the complex problems that matter.
[...]
Modern Tech Stack: We usually build our services using the latest Python tooling and FastAPI, dockerize them and deploy with CI/CD to K8s in AWS. We keep the code on GitHub.

results.title: Senior / Principal Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
highlights:
Senior / Principal Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
[...]
Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche’s Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness the transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide. ​
[...]
At Roche’s AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning (ML) techniques. We are seeking a Senior or Principal Machine Learning Scientist to join the Foundation Models team within Prescient Design (gRED). In this role, you will drive the development of our internal reasoning Large Language Models (LLMs) and enable it to succeed at drug discovery tasks, including biomolecular design. You will work at the intersection of engineering and research, designing and scaling large machine learning systems.
[...]
design and evolution
[...]
Domain Translation: Translate biological and chemical domain knowledge into machine learning objectives, training signals, and evaluation criteria, working closely with domain experts.
[...]
Scalable Systems & Engineering: Architect and improve large-scale distributed machine learning systems, ensuring robustness, efficiency, and reproducibility across training and evaluation workflows.
[...]
As a Principal ML Scientist:
[...]
You help define the long-term technical roadmap for scientific reasoning models, identifying new opportunities and setting priorities across initiatives.
[...]
You architect new initiatives
[...]
data modalities, guiding the technical direction of cross-functional projects across gRED.
[...]
You serve as a key technical authority for leadership, influencing how Genentech leverages generative AI to solve high-stakes problems in the therapeutic pipeline.
[...]
For Senior (SE6): 0 – 2+ years of industry or post-doc experience with a focus on deep learning.
[...]
For Principal (SE7): 5+ years of industry or post-PhD experience with a demonstrated track record of technical leadership and project ownership.
[...]
LLM Expertise: Extensive experience developing and training large-scale machine learning models, including approaches to improve domain understanding, reasoning capabilities, and model alignment.
[...]
Engineering: Strong software engineering skills and experience designing and operating large-scale or high-performance machine learning systems.
[...]
Experience with molecular modalities (e.g., protein sequences, chemical graphs, and structured molecular data) is highly valued but not required.
[...]
A public portfolio of research or significant contributions to open-source ML libraries.
[...]
A passion for applying frontier AI to drug discovery.
[...]
The expected salary range for this position, based on the primary location of San Francisco, is $167,400 – 310,800 of hiring range for the Senior Scientist, and $194,000 – 360,400 for the Principal Scientist. For the primary of location of New York City, $160,100 – 297,300 for the Senior Scientist, and $185,600 – 344,600 for the Principal Scientist. Actual pay will be determined based on experience, qualifications, geographic location, and other job-related factors permitted by law. A discretionary annual bonus may be available based on individual and Company performance. This position also qualifies for the benefits detailed at the link provided below.

results.title: Senior Machine Learning Scientist – NLP People
highlights:
Senior Machine Learning Scientist – NLP People
[...]
Senior Data Scientist
[...]
🦸‍♀️ The Role 🦸‍♀️
[...]
Our mission is to help large successful brands like Uber, Amazon, Wise, HelloFresh (and more!) put their customers at the centre of everything they do. Using best-in-class tech in a fast-developing AI space, our Customer Experience Intelligence platform continuously analyses explicit and implicit feedback to enable our clients to identify what they should do next.
[...]
We’re hiring a Senior Data Scientist to join the team and help build and ship the next generation of that stack.
[...]
Unlike many companies, we use our own custom models, specialised for customer feedback, across various parts of the stack: extraction, retrieval, reranking, summarisation, and sentiment analysis. We are also pragmatic and understand that the right solution can be a combination of off-the-shelf LLMs, bespoke fine-tuned models, and sometimes techniques that utilise no LLM at all.
[...]
Train, evaluate, and iterate on ML models and agentic systems for customer feedback, including owning our custom fine-tuning pipelines. Run experiments end-to-end, track results rigorously, and make clear recommendations on what to ship, iterate, or retire.
[...]
Build and maintain LLM-powered features: retrieval pipelines, reranking systems, insight agents, data mining agents, and automated taxonomy generation.
[...]
Design and run robust evaluation frameworks: build test sets, define metrics, evaluate non-deterministic systems, handle class imbalance, and automate checkpoint comparisons.
[...]
Improve and extend semantic search and retrieval, evolving from embedding-based approaches toward more advanced methods.
[...]
Write production-quality code and collaborate closely with Engineering on productionisation, model serving, data pipelines, and monitoring.
[...]
Work with Product and Commercial teams to translate business needs into practical ML solutions, and support client evaluations and accuracy benchmarking.
[...]
🧰 What you’ll need:
[...]
A deep working knowledge of transformer architectures.
[...]
Strong PyTorch skills, with the ability to write custom training loops, modify model architectures, and debug issues at the tensor level. Ideally, experience with parameter-efficient fine-tuning techniques such as LoRA
[...]
Extensive experience working with large-scale, messy real-world text data, including classification, extraction, embeddings, re-rankers, clustering, and search.
[...]
Experience in instruction fine-tuning and serving language models, familiarity with frameworks such as vLLM, DeepSpeed, or similar tools
[...]
A solid grounding in classical ML and statistics, and the judgement to choose simpler methods when they’re the right solution.
[...]
Practical experience building with GenAI and agentic patterns.
[...]
Technical curiosity and a keen interest in AI – a love of experimenting to make the most of available technology.
[...]
MSc/PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Computational Linguistics or a closely related STEM field.
[...]
Experience with reinforcement learning techniques, such as with verifiable reward (RLVR)
[...]
